commit.message:
Remove silent source file exclusion during Roslyn compilation (#80)

* Remove silent source file exclusion during Roslyn compilation

Previously, when Roslyn compilation failed, the runner would silently
retry by dropping files with direct errors and recompiling the remainder.
This could produce a passing run that was missing whole codeunits — a
silent false positive that is hard to detect and debug.

Now compilation is all-or-nothing: any error causes an immediate hard
failure with all Roslyn errors printed to stderr. Users get a clear
signal that compilation failed and exactly which files/lines are broken.

Changes:
- Remove RoslynCompiler.ExcludedFiles dictionary
- Remove RoslynCompiler.GetExclusionInfo() method
- Remove iterative 5-round exclusion retry loop in CompileFromTrees
- Compilation errors now always print to stderr (not verbose-only)
- Remove GetExclusionInfo() call sites in MockCodeunitHandle and
  MockRecordHandle (replaced with plain 'not found' messages)
- Add two unit tests in PipelineTests:
  RoslynCompiler_MultipleFiles_ReturnsNullWhenAnyFileHasErrors
  RoslynCompiler_MultipleFiles_ReturnsNullWhenSecondFileHasErrors

Closes #66

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review feedback: clean up ExcludedFiles remnants and test quality

- Remove all remaining RoslynCompiler.ExcludedFiles usages in Program.cs
  (PrintCompilationGaps call and TryReportPipelineGapsAsync argument)
- Remove Executor.PrintCompilationGaps() method (now dead code)
- Update TelemetryReporter.TryReportPipelineGapsAsync to drop the
  compilationGaps parameter and all compilation-gap telemetry logic
- Make RoslynCompiler.Compile(List<(Name,SyntaxTree)>) internal so the
  test project no longer needs explicit Microsoft.CodeAnalysis References
- Remove fragile bin-path Microsoft.CodeAnalysis Reference items from
  AlRunner.Tests.csproj
- Print all Roslyn errors on compilation failure (remove Take(30) cap)
- Rename 'RED tests' comment to 'Regression tests for Issue #66'
- Remove .cs extension from test source Name values (Compile appends .cs)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix CI: run tests individually; remove XMLPort from test 13; improve regression test ordering

- Update test-matrix.yml to run each test case individually using a for loop,
  matching the documented usage in CLAUDE.md. The previous single-invocation
  approach was relying on the now-removed exclusion loop to silently drop
  duplicate object IDs that exist across different test suites (e.g. tests 01,
  02, 79 all define codeunit 50900/50901/50902).

- Remove ItemStagingXmlPort.al from tests/13-partial-compile/src. That file
  existed solely to demonstrate the old partial-compile (exclusion) behaviour,
  which has been removed. The test now covers table + codeunit compilation only
  and can be included in the standard CI loop.

- Rename the second Issue #66 regression test to
  RoslynCompiler_MultipleFiles_ReturnsNullWhenFirstFileHasErrors and swap the
  file order so invalid comes before valid, covering the scenario the original
  second test was duplicating.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: distinguish compilation errors from test failures in --output-json (#67) (#81)

* fix: distinguish compilation errors from test failures in --output-json

Fixes #67.

When a codeunit or record type is excluded from Roslyn compilation (e.g.
because it depends on an XmlPort or other unsupported BC object type), the
runner previously reported the resulting runtime exception as status: "fail"
in the JSON output. This was misleading: "fail" should mean an assertion
failure in test logic, not a tooling/configuration problem.

Changes:
- Add CompilationExcludedException (AlRunner.Runtime namespace), thrown by
  MockCodeunitHandle and MockRecordHandle when a compiled-out type is invoked.
- Executor.RunTests catches CompilationExcludedException alongside
  NotSupportedException and maps both to TestStatus.Error instead of Fail.
- SerializeJsonOutput: status "error" is emitted for Error results; the
  top-level "errors" counter is populated; "failed" now counts only real
  assertion failures.
- New top-level "compilationErrors" array in JSON output lists each excluded
  C# file and its Roslyn diagnostics (omitted when empty/null).
- Pipeline.RunCore passes RoslynCompiler.ExcludedFiles to SerializeJsonOutput.
- Server.cs SerializeServerResponse includes compilationErrors field.
- --help and --guide updated with status value documentation.
- Test case tests/84-json-compilation-error: XmlPort alongside a passing
  codeunit; verifies compilationErrors is populated in JSON output.
- AlRunner.Tests/JsonOutputTests.cs: 9 unit/integration tests.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix review: don't leak stale compilationErrors on server cache hits

- Server.cs: only populate compilationErrors when cached=false; on cache
  hits ExcludedFiles holds data from a prior compile and would be misleading
- JsonOutputTests.cs: rename failStatuses -> errorStatuses for clarity
- OrderCalculatorTest.al: rename TotalWithTax_WrongResult_Fails ->
  TotalWithTax_WrongExpectedValue_AssertError to better reflect intent

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address review comments in server compilation error tracking

- Comment 1: store compilationErrors in CompilationCache alongside the
  assembly; on cache hits return stored errors instead of reading stale
  RoslynCompiler.ExcludedFiles from a previous request
- Comment 2: rename local variable failStatuses -> errorStatuses in
  JsonOutputTests to match intent (collecting error-status tests)
- Comment 3: rename test procedure TotalWithTax_WrongResult_Fails ->
  TotalWithTax_WrongExpectedValue_AssertError for clarity
- Comment 4 (TDD): add Server_CacheHit_CompilationErrors_MatchOriginalCompilation
  test that verifies compilationErrors is present and correct on both
  cache-miss and cache-hit responses

feat: add MockXmlPortHandle so XmlPort variables compile in al-runner (#83)

* feat: add MockXmlPortHandle stub so XmlPort variables compile

NavXmlPortHandle was missing Source, Destination, Import, Export, and
Invoke members after the existing .Target-stripping rewrite, causing CS1061
compile errors in any codeunit that uses an XmlPort variable.

Changes:
- Add AlRunner/Runtime/MockXmlPortHandle.cs: stub for NavXmlPortHandle.
  Exposes Source (MockInStream?) and Destination (MockOutStream?) properties
  to satisfy the field assignments the BC compiler emits after .Target is
  stripped. Instance Import/Export throw NotSupportedException with a clear
  injection hint. Static StaticImport/StaticExport handle the XmlPort.Import/
  Export(portId, stream) static call form. Invoke() returns null.
- Update RoslynRewriter.cs with three rewrite rules:
  1. VisitIdentifierName: NavXmlPortHandle -> MockXmlPortHandle
  2. VisitObjectCreationExpression: strip ITreeObject arg from
     new MockXmlPortHandle(this, id) -> new MockXmlPortHandle(id)
  3. VisitInvocationExpression: NavXmlPort.Import/Export(errorLevel, id,
     stream, ...) -> MockXmlPortHandle.StaticImport/Export(...)
- Add tests/84-xmlport/ with 6 test cases (2 positive, 4 negative) covering
  instance and static Import/Export on both instance and static call forms.
- Update CLAUDE.md, README.md, Program.cs (--guide), and CHANGELOG.md.

Fixes #62

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolve DataError compile error and address review comments

- Replace DataError (BC-specific type) with object? in MockXmlPortHandle
  Import/Export/StaticImport/StaticExport — fixes CS0246 compile error
- Remove stray unconditional RunCodeunit rewrite block that was copy-pasted
  after the NavXmlPort.Import/Export rule in RoslynRewriter
- Fix MockXmlPortHandle 1-arg constructor stripping to only strip when the
  single argument is 'this' (avoids dropping the xmlPortId)
- Add ALAssign to MockOutStream so OutStream variable assignment compiles
- README: fix duplicate item 3 numbering; clarify XmlPort partially supported
- XmlPortLogic.al: update GetXmlPortId() comment to correctly describe
  what the test proves (compilation smoke-test, not handle ID verification)

---------

packages to re-attach the OTel span as the current context during
streaming iteration, ensuring child spans created by framework

agentserver invoke_agent span instead of appearing as siblings.

overridable function. The default _default_configure_observability
attaches a console StreamHandler to the root logger (so user

control.

Implement well-formed JSON.stringify for lone surrogates

JSON.stringify now escapes lone surrogates (U+D800..U+DFFF) as \uXXXX
sequences instead of outputting replacement characters. The string
serializer now operates on raw UTF-16 code units to correctly detect
and handle unpaired surrogates while preserving valid surrogate pairs.

ci(tooling): Use GH_COPILOT_TOKEN for GitHub Models API call

The release workflow was authenticating against the GitHub Models API
with the built-in GITHUB_TOKEN, which does not carry the Copilot
entitlements needed to reach the models.github.ai endpoint. Requests
were therefore rejected with a 401/403, causing the LLM-generated
release-notes step to fall back to the minimal plaintext list on every
run.

Switching to GH_COPILOT_TOKEN — a repository secret backed by a PAT
(or a fine-grained token) that has GitHub Copilot access — gives the
workflow the correct identity. Using the default GITHUB_TOKEN was not
viable because its permission set is fixed by GitHub and cannot include
Copilot model access regardless of how the workflow's permissions block
is configured.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Alexandre Nicolaie <xunleii@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: extract _BaseExchangeAdapter to eliminate ~60% adapter duplication (#22)

* refactor: extract _BaseExchangeAdapter and fix ValidationRunner dead code

Exchange adapters (nordpool.py, epex_spot.py) shared ~60% of their
implementation verbatim. Extract the common behaviour into
_BaseExchangeAdapter in exchanges/base.py:

- capabilities property
- get_products() — 96 QH product IDs using self._area_id prefix
- get_orderbook() — empty OrderBook (matching engine owns state)
- submit_order() / cancel_order() — raise NotImplementedError
- validate_order() — checks ExchangeCapabilities limits, uses
  self._display_name in error messages

NordPoolAdapter and EpexSpotAdapter now inherit _BaseExchangeAdapter and
only define __init__ (ExchangeCapabilities + super().__init__ call) and
gate_closure_offset (which differs between exchanges). Line counts:
nordpool.py 52 -> 24 stmts, epex_spot.py 47 -> 20 stmts.

ValidationRunner.run() pre-created checker instances in the remaining[]
list (all assigned to _checker and immediately discarded), then
re-imported and re-instantiated them inside an if/elif chain. Replace
with Callable[[], StepResult] lambdas — one entry per step, no dead
objects, no duplicated import aliases.

Update GUIDE-NEW-EXCHANGES.md Step 2 template to match the new
inheritance pattern. Add a table of what _BaseExchangeAdapter provides
for free, document the three super().__init__ arguments, and update the
naive-datetime common mistake note (get_orderbook is now in the base).

commit.message:
fix(build): sign release APKs with debug keystore for sideloading

Release APKs were unsigned, causing Android to refuse installation.
Add signingConfig = signingConfigs.getByName("debug") to the release
build type so release APKs are signed with the default debug keystore.

This enables sideloading of minified/shrunk release builds. The debug
keystore is auto-generated by the Android SDK on all machines (local
and CI), so no secrets or keystore files are needed.

commit.message:
fix: remove duplicate `list-instances` entry from CLI help (#4542)

The `list_instances` command was registered twice:
- Once automatically via `@main.command()` decorator (as `list-instances`)
- Once explicitly via `main.add_command(list_instances, name='list')` in `__init__.py`

This caused both `list` and `list-instances` to appear in `pymapdl --help`.

Fix by changing the decorator to `@click.command()` so the command is only
registered once (as `list`) through the explicit `add_command` call in
`__init__.py`. Also removes the now-unnecessary `from ansys.mapdl.core.cli
import main` import.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
